title: GENERAL STANDARD FOR SOY PROTEIN PRODUCTS

sections.section: GENERAL STANDARD FOR SOY PROTEIN PRODUCTS
sections.summary: Title only — identifies the document as the General Standard for Soy Protein Products (no substantive provisions in this section).
Identifies the standard as CXS 175-1989, adopted in 1989 and amended in 2019 and 2022.

Records a 2022 amendment replacing the previous text on labelling of non-retail containers with a requirement that such labelling should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Applies this Standard to vegetable protein products prepared from soybeans by separation/extraction processes intended for foods requiring further preparation and for use by the food processing industry.

Defines Soy Protein Products (SPP) by protein content on a dry weight basis excluding added vitamins/minerals/amino acids/additives: soy protein flour (SPF) 50%–<65%, soy protein concentrate (SPC) 65%–<90%, and soy protein isolate (SPI) 90% or more.

sections.section: 3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY AND NUTRITIONAL FACTORS
sections.summary: Specifies raw material quality and compositional/nutritional requirements: raw materials must be clean, sound, mature, dry seeds or compliant SPP; sets limits including moisture ≤10%, ash ≤8% d.w., protein ranges by product type, and crude fibre limits (SPF ≤5%, SPC ≤6%, SPI ≤0.5%), lists optional ingredients, and requires controlled processing to secure flavour/palatability and control trypsin inhibitor/hemagglutinins without appreciably impairing nutritive value.

Requires raw materials to be clean, sound, mature, dry seeds essentially free from other seeds and foreign matter in accordance with Good Manufacturing Practice, or SPP of lower protein content meeting the Standard's specifications.

sections.section: 3.2 SPP shall conform to the following compositional requirements
sections.summary: Sets SPP compositional requirements: moisture ≤10% (m/m); crude protein ranges by product (SPF 50–<65%, SPC 65–<90%, SPI ≥90% on dry basis); ash ≤8% d.w.; residual fat compatible with GMP; and crude fibre limits (SPF ≤5%, SPC ≤6%, SPI ≤0.5%).
Specifies that moisture content shall not exceed 10% (m/m).
Specifies crude protein (N x 6.25) ranges for SPP: SPF 50%–<65%, SPC 65%–<90%, SPI 90% or more.

sections.section: 3.2.2.1 -in the case of SPF, 50% or more and less than 65%
sections.summary: Defines soy protein flour (SPF) as having crude protein (N x 6.25) of 50% or more and less than 65% on a dry weight basis.

sections.section: 3.2.2.2 -in the case of SPC, 65% or more and less than 90%
sections.summary: Defines soy protein concentrate (SPC) as having crude protein (N x 6.25) of 65% or more and less than 90% on a dry weight basis.

sections.section: 3.2.2.3 -in the case of SPI, 90% or more
sections.summary: Defines soy protein isolate (SPI) as having crude protein (N x 6.25) of 90% or more on a dry weight basis.
Ash (yield on incineration) shall not exceed 8% on a dry weight basis.
Residual fat content must be compatible with Good Manufacturing Practice (no numeric limit specified).

sections.section: 3.2.5 Crude fibre content shall not exceed
sections.summary: Crude fibre content limits on a dry weight basis are specified by product type: SPF ≤5%, SPC ≤6% and SPI ≤0.5%.
For SPF, crude fibre content shall not exceed 5% on a dry weight basis.
For SPC, crude fibre content shall not exceed 6% on a dry weight basis.
For SPI, crude fibre content shall not exceed 0.5% on a dry weight basis.
Optional ingredients permitted include carbohydrates (including sugars), edible fats and oils, other protein products, vitamins and minerals, salt, and herbs and spices.

Processing must be controlled to ensure optimum flavour/palatability and to control factors such as trypsin inhibitor and hemagglutinins appropriate to intended use, with maximum trypsin inhibitor levels defined for the finished product where necessary; special-purpose SPP produced under low-temperature conditions shall be assayed for protein nutritive value after appropriate heat treatment and processing must not be so severe as to appreciably impair nutritive value.
Section heading only for Food Additives; no substantive provisions in this section.

During manufacture of SPP certain classes of processing aids may be used, consistent with the Guidelines on Substances used as Processing Aids (CXG 75-2010); listed classes include acidity regulators, antifoam agents, firming agents, enzyme preparations, extraction solvents, antidusting agents, flour treatment agents and viscosity control agents.
No food additives are permitted in vegetable protein products (prohibition).
SPP shall be free from heavy metals in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.

Production is recommended to follow the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969); products should be free from objectionable matter to the extent of GMP and, when tested, must not contain microorganisms, substances from microorganisms, or other poisonous substances in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.
SPP shall be packed in suitable hygienic containers that maintain the product in a dry and sanitary condition during storage and transport.
Labelling of these products must comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).

The food name declared must reflect protein content thresholds: 'soy protein flour' for ≥50% and <65%, 'soy protein concentrate' for ≥65% and <90%, and 'soy protein isolate' (or equivalent) for ≥90%; the name may also include terms describing physical form (e.g., 'granules') or processing such as 'textured'.

A complete list of ingredients shall be declared on the label in descending order of proportion, except added vitamins and minerals may be shown as separate groups and need not be listed within those groups in descending order.
Labelling of non-retail containers should conform to the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Compliance with this Standard shall be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions.
